definition: Percentile rank of the selected diagnostic-passing model within its dataset and primary metric. Slider heldout ELPD is preferred when available; production uses LOO ELPD.
Average of the normalized PPC RMSE score and PPC R2 percentile rank within diagnostic-passing rows for the dataset; higher is better.
Manual theoretical-alignment score for how much the candidate can be described as one model family across datasets, allowing only response-space reductions.
Applied when a slider candidate is available only through full-sample LOO rather than heldout ELPD.
